sections.paragraph:
ἀμβλύνω 1 aor. ptc. ἀμβλύνας (Emped. Fgm. 2, 2 Diels; Trag., Thu.; Gen. 127:1; Joseph.) prim. ‘make blunt’ e.g. a sharp instrument, in our lit. only metaph. make dim ἀστέρα … λάμψαντα ἐν τοῖς ἄστροις τούτοις καὶ ἀμβλύναντα αὐτούς a star that shined among these stars and dimmed their light GJs 21:2 (codd.).—DELG s.v. ἀμβλύς.

aor. aor. = aorist

ptc. ptc. = participle

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

prim. prim. = primarily

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

metaph. metaph. = metaphor(ically)

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 52
